theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 2: The Performance Pro + Archetype 4: Swiss Brutalism
theme_and_archetype.reasoning: High-energy, authoritative, data-dense. Avoids the clichéd 'F1 Red' and 'AI Purple'. Relies on Deep Obsidian and Volt/Cyber Yellow for maximum contrast and an aggressive, tactical motorsport journalism feel.

hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl tracking-tighter uppercase font-black
hierarchy.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl tracking-tight uppercase font-extrabold

rules: CRITICAL: Do NOT use Inter, Roboto, or standard sans. Install fonts from Google Fonts/Fontshare or use standard fallback 'sans-serif' if unavailable. Prefer Cabinet Grotesk for headings, Manrope for body, and JetBrains Mono for data grids.
Use JetBrains Mono for all numeric data (lap times, points, years) to ensure tabular alignment.
Headings must have tight letter spacing.
No full-screen gradients. Use flat dark colors with sharp 1px borders.
Text over images MUST have text shadow or a dark gradient overlay.
Volt Yellow (#E4FF00) is used sparingly for primary actions (Simulate button) and highlighting the simulated champion in tables.

spacing_and_layout.spacing_scale: Use generous padding for sections (py-24), but tight padding for data components (p-4, p-6).

season_selector.description: A dense, tactical numerical grid or timeline slider to select years (1950-2025).
season_selector.styling: Use JetBrains Mono. Selected year has bg-[#E4FF00] text-black. Unselected years have text-[#A3A3A3] hover:text-white hover:bg-[#1F1F1F].

standings_table.styling: Control Room Grid. 1px solid border-b border-[#262626] per row. No rounded corners. Highlight the alternate champion row in #E4FF00 text with a subtle #E4FF00/10 background. Roster data is left-aligned, points are right-aligned.

race_timeline.description: Vertical line on the left side displaying the season's calendar.
race_timeline.styling: Border-l-2 border-[#262626]. Active/completed races have a bright #00F0FF node. Contains small cards with podium results.

news_bento.description: Grid of AI-generated articles in Portuguese (e.g. 'Manchetes da Temporada').
news_bento.styling: Bento Grid Mode A (Tetris). Mixture of square and tall cards. Large headline layered over a dark-tinted image (bg-black/50 overlay). Hovering scales the image slightly and brightens the border.

simulate_button.styling: bg-[#E4FF00] text-black font-black uppercase tracking-[0.2em] px-8 py-4 rounded-none hover:bg-[#C6DB00] transition-colors border-2 border-transparent focus:border-white.

motion_and_interaction.hover_states: Cards lift slightly (-translate-y-1). Image overlays go from black/60 to black/30. Borders glow.
motion_and_interaction.page_transitions: Staggered fade-up for table rows and news bento items.
motion_and_interaction.simulate_action: When simulation is running, display a retro-futuristic terminal loader with rapid scrolling data (ASCII or mono text) before revealing the alternate history.

media_and_images.instructions: Use these exact URLs for hero sections, news article backgrounds, and driver placeholders. Do not use generic placeholders like via.placeholder.com. ONLY use these URLs.

hero_and_backgrounds.description: F1 car on track - Use as the main hero background with a heavy black/60 overlay.
F1 car red/black - Use for major dramatic news articles.

news_thumbnails.description: Driver in white helmet - Focus piece news article
Helmet close up - Championship victory article

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure white or Volt Yellow text has at least 4.5:1 ratio against #0A0A0A or #141414.
accessibility_and_testing.testing: All interactive elements (buttons, selectors, timeline nodes, etc.) MUST include data-testid attributes in kebab-case format (e.g., data-testid="simulate-button", data-testid="season-selector-1994").
